summary: The OpenSpec change is well-formed and meets all review criteria. The proposal, design, spec, and tasks documents are coherent, consistent, and correctly structured.

less_noisy_overview_and_timeline_focused.notes: Proposal, design, and spec all explicitly require removing noisy defaults. The design defines a normalized info model with explicit action, handoff, and reason_for_handoff fields. Spec scenarios require actionable context over raw payloads. Tasks 1.1, 1.3, 1.4, and 1.6 map to this.

preserve_same_agent_back_and_forth_excha.notes: Proposal calls out repeated exchanges as meaningful operational evidence. Design states the display layer must not deduplicate solely by agent name, handoff target, action text, or status. Spec has a dedicated scenario 'Duplicate same-agent exchanges are preserved' with precise given/when/then conditions. Task 1.5 covers this explicitly.

current_nicegui_components.notes: Design names specific NiceGUI components (ui.table, ui.tabs, ui.tab_panels, ui.expansion, chips, badges, splitters, dialogs). Spec adds a dedicated 'Modern NiceGUI Information Layout' requirement with three BDD scenarios. Task 1.7 covers the rework.

preserve_read_only_api_live_update_contr.notes: Proposal excludes write operations, auth changes, and source contract mutations from scope. Design has an explicit compatibility and safety section requiring backward-compatible additions only. Spec scenarios 'Overview remains source backed and read only' and 'Timeline fields are normalized compatibly' enforce this. Task 1.10 requires endpoint compatibility checks.

desktop_mobile_no_overspill_verification.notes: Design has a responsiveness section and verification strategy covering desktop and mobile widths with representative long content. Spec has dedicated 'Desktop layout supports dense scanning without overflow' and 'Mobile layout preserves primary information' scenarios. Tasks 1.8 and 1.9 explicitly require these verification checks.

protocol_compliance.notes: All four required documents are present (proposal.md, design.md, specs/web-app-hub/spec.md, tasks.md). Spec uses correct MODIFIED/ADDED delta structure. Requirements follow BDD given/when/then format. Scope section clearly defines in-scope and out-of-scope boundaries.

scope_fit.notes: Change is bounded to web_app_hub.py operator UI surfaces. Out-of-scope correctly excludes authentication, write operations, non-operator views, Hub/MCP/Kubernetes/OpenSpec contract changes, and live-update replacement.

validation_readiness.notes: Task 1.10 requires focused no-spend tests for normalization, duplicate preservation, concise defaults, endpoint compatibility, and responsive layout. Tasks 1.8 and 1.9 require desktop and mobile rendering checks. Task 1.11 requires OpenSpec validation. Task 1.12 requires static checks and fixture tests.

acceptance_quality.notes: All documents are present, internally consistent, and cross-referencing. Information model in design.md is precise and implementation-actionable. Tasks are numbered and traceable to requirements. No ambiguities or gaps identified.
